title: Dictionary for Interpreting Behavior Control and Modification

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: Execute a series of child nodes in order from left to right.
nodes.behaviorTemplate: Execute [Actions] in order. If [Action] fails, stop sequence. If [Action] is RUNNING, wait for [Action] to finish. Else move to the next [Action].

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: Execute a series of child nodes in order, from left to right, remembering which ones have already succeeded.
nodes.behaviorTemplate: Execute [Actions] in sequence. If [action] returns SUCCESS, remember it and move to the next. If [Action] returns FAILURE, start the sequence again from the beginning.

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: Execute child node based on blackboard value.
nodes.behaviorTemplate: Read blackboard value. Compare value against a set of cases. If case value matches switch value execute corresponding [Action]. Else if no case matches, execute default [Action].

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: Execute a node while a condition is true else execute and alternative node if that same condition fails.
nodes.behaviorTemplate: While [Condition], do [Action]. If [!Condition], execute alternative [Action].

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: Repeatedly execute a child node for a specified number of iterations, until a specific status is returned or forever.
nodes.behaviorTemplate: Execute [Action] continuously until a FAILURE or SUCCESS is returned. Else return RUNNING and keep executing [Action] forever.

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: Execute multiple child nodes simultaneously, ensuring all are completed successfully.
nodes.behaviorTemplate: Execute a series of [Actions] simultaneously. If success threshold is reached, return SUCCESS. Else if failure threshold is reached, return FAILURE. Else return RUNNING.

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: Execute child nodes sequentially from left to right, until one succeeds or all fail.
nodes.behaviorTemplate: Execute [Actions] in order. If [Action] fails, move to the next [Action] until any [Action] succeeds. Then return SUCCESS. If no [Action] succeeds, return FAILURE.

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: Force child node to succeed regardless of the conditions.
nodes.behaviorTemplate: Execute [Action] but make sure it always returns SUCCESS, unless the [Action] is still RUNNING.

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: Force child node to fail regardless of the conditions.
nodes.behaviorTemplate: Execute [Action] but make sure it always returns FAILURE when it is complete.

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: Repeatedly execute child node until success is achieved.
nodes.behaviorTemplate: Continuously execute [Action] until it succeeds. Then stop [Action] execution and return SUCCESS.

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: Retry the execution of a child node for either a fixed number of times or until the node is executed successfully.
nodes.behaviorTemplate: Continuously execute [Action] whenever it fails. Repeat for a specified retry count or until SUCCESS is returned.

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: Execute a series of child nodes in order from left to right. While a later node is running, re-evaluate previous child nodes.
nodes.behaviorTemplate: Execute [Actions] in order. If [Action] returns SUCCESS, move to the next. If [Action] fails, stop and return FAILURE. If [Action] is RUNNING, return RUNNING but re-evaluate previous [Actions] in order.

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: Execute a series of child nodes in order from left to right. While a later node is running, restart evaluation from node 0.
nodes.behaviorTemplate: Execute a series of [Actions] in order. If [Action] returns SUCCESS, stop execution. Else keep running and re-evaluate previous [Actions] in order, whenever an [Action] is in execution.

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: Execute a set of child nodes multiple times.
nodes.behaviorTemplate: Execute [Action] a set number of times or infinitely.

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: Check if a value in memory can be either true or false matches the expected value.
nodes.behaviorTemplate: Look up a value in the blackboard. If the value matches target, return SUCCESS. Else return FAILURE. Also, if the blackboard does not have required key return FAILURE.

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: Wait for a given duration before running or returning a result from the associated child node.
nodes.behaviorTemplate: Execute [Action]. If the [Action] returns SUCCESS, start delay timer. While delay is active, return RUNNING. After delay, return SUCCESS.

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: Negate a condition.
nodes.behaviorTemplate: Execute an [Action]. If [Action] returns SUCCESS or FAILURE return the reverse. Else return RUNNING.

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: Keep re-running child node as long as it does not fail.
nodes.behaviorTemplate: Execute [Action]. If [Action] returns SUCCESS, retart it and keep running. Else if [Action] returns FAILURE, stop looping and return SUCCESS. Else return RUNNING.

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: Continuously retry a child node until it succeeds.
nodes.behaviorTemplate: Keep executing [Action] until it returns SUCCESS. If retry returns FAILURE, restart [Action]. Else return RUNNING.

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: Execute child node, but only for a maximum duration.
nodes.behaviorTemplate: If the execution of [Action] does not end within a specified duration, interrupt it and return a specific result.

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: A child node name
nodes.behaviorTemplate: If action name is a standard ROS action, refer to it as: ROS action [insert action name]. Else refer to it as: custom action [insert action name]

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: A series of child node names
nodes.behaviorTemplate: For each action name, if it is a standard ROS action, refer to it as: ROS action [insert action name]. Else refer to it as: custom action [insert action name]

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: A check that returns success
nodes.behaviorTemplate: [condition ID] is true

nodes.naturalLanguageDescription: A check that returns failure
nodes.behaviorTemplate: [condition ID] is false
